zh.lifeSummary: 當Ida or Editha de Criol於1327年出生在英國肯特麥德斯同時，她的父親Sir John de Criol22歲，母親Lettice17歲。她於1340年與John Brockhull在英國英格蘭肯特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1383年10月16日在英國肯特坎特布瑞享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ida or Editha de Criol于1327年出生在英国肯特麥德斯同时，她的父亲Sir John de Criol22岁，母亲Lettice17岁。她于1340年与John Brockhull在英国英格兰肯特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1383年10月16日在英国肯特坎特布瑞享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Criol
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of German   Krill   or   Grill  . This surname is also found in the Channel Islands.
English and Scottish: metathesized form of   Curl  .

variants.name: Krill
meanings: nickname for a lively, cheerful person, from Low German krill ‘lively, healthy’.

nameMeanings.name: Ida
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Norman name, of Germanic origin, derived from īd ‘work’. This died out during the later Middle Ages. It was revived in the 19th century, influenced by its use in Tennyson's The Princess ( 1847 ) for the central character, who devotes herself to the cause of women's rights and women's education in a thoroughly Victorian way. The name is also associated with Mount Ida in Crete, which was connected in classical times with the worship of Zeus, king of the gods, who was supposed to have been brought up in a cave on the mountainside. In the 1930s it became famous as the name of the film star Ida Lupino ( 1914–1995 ).

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally the name of the god of thunder in Norse mythology, Thor (Old Norse Þórr). It was not used as a personal name during the Middle Ages. The modern name is either a late 18th-century revival of the god's name or else a vernacular development of Tord .

variants.name: Thor
meanings: Originally the name of the god of thunder in Norse mythology, Thor (Old Norse Þórr). It was not used as a personal name during the Middle Ages. The modern name is either a late 18th-century revival of the god's name or else a vernacular development of Tord .

nameMeanings.name: Editha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English female personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + gӯð ‘strife’. This was borne by a daughter ( 961–984 ) of Edgar the Peaceful. (She was named in accordance with the common Old English practice of repeating name elements within a family.) She spent her short life in a convent, and is regarded as a saint.

variants.name: Edythe
meanings: From an Old English female personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + gӯð ‘strife’. This was borne by a daughter ( 961–984 ) of Edgar the Peaceful. (She was named in accordance with the common Old English practice of repeating name elements within a family.) She spent her short life in a convent, and is regarded as a saint.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Ida or Editha de Criol was born in 1327, in Maidstone, Kent, England, her father, Sir John de Criol, was 22 and her mother, Lettice, was 17. She married John Brockhull in 1340, in Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 16 October 1383, in Canterbury, Kent, England, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ida or Editha de Criol est née en 1327, à Maidstone, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Sir John de Criol, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Lettice, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé John Brockhull en 1340, à Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 16 octobre 1383, à Canterbury, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ida or Editha de Criol 1327 in Maidstone, Kent, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John de Criol 22 und ihre Mutter Lettice 17. Sie hat 1340 in Kent, England, United Kingdom John Brockhull geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 16. Oktober 1383 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Canterbury, Kent, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ida or Editha de Criol è nata il nel 1327 nel Maidstone, Kent, England, sua padre, Sir John de Criol, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Lettice, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato John Brockhull nel 1340, nel Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 16 ottobre 1383, nel Canterbury, Kent, England, all'età di 56 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Ida or Editha de Criolが1327年にイギリスケントメイドストーンで生まれた時，父Sir John de Criolは22歳，母Letticeは17歳であった。1340年にイギリスイギリスケントでJohn Brockhullと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1383年10月16日にイギリスケントカンテルブリーで56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Ida or Editha de Criol(이)가 1327년 영국 켄트 메이드스톤에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John de Criol(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lettice(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1340년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트에서 John Brockhull(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1383년 10월 16일 영국 켄트 캉르버이에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ida or Editha de Criol nasceu em 1327, em Maidstone, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John de Criol, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Lettice, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com John Brockhull em 1340, em Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 16 de outubro de 1383, em Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ida or Editha de Criol родилась в 1327 г. в местности меидстоун, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John de Criol было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Lettice, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Brockhull в 1340 г. в местности кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 16 октября 1383 г. в местности кантирбури, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 56 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ida or Editha de Criol nació en 1327, en Maidstone, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John de Criol, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Lettice, tenía 17 años. Se casó con John Brockhull en 1340, en Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 16 de octubre de 1383, en Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 56 años.
